item.title: RFK Jr. Congressional Hearing & FDA
item.description:
• Summary: RFK Jr.'s congressional testimony addresses the FDA's role and regulatory policies under the Trump administration's health agenda. Discussions focus on balancing public health goals with political priorities, including debates over the FDA's independence and potential reforms, with RFK Jr. defending the agency against Republican scrutiny.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News, STAT News, Endpoints News, BioPharma Dive

item.title: FDA & Regulatory Policy (Peptides/Compounding)
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA is considering new restrictions on compounding pharmacies for peptides, which have been flagged for safety risks. An advisory committee reviews the implications for Alzheimer's drugs and other compounded peptides, aiming to tighten regulations and address concerns over unapproved formulations.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: BioSpace, STAT News

item.title: AI in Biopharma
item.description:
• Summary: OpenAI launches a biopharma-focused AI model to compete with Anthropic, while Novartis CEO joins Anthropic's board, embedding AI in drug discovery. The blurring lines of healthcare AI include voice chatbots for mental health and integration into consumer devices, driving a medical AI revolution.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News, BioSpace, Endpoints News, STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Drug Pricing & FDA Transparency
item.description:
• Summary: Articles highlight calls from drug pricing watchdogs for increased transparency into the FDA's accelerated approval decisions, with Moderna's case serving as a cautionary tale on premium pricing models and regulatory oversight.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Biospace, Endpoints News

item.title: Viatris & Teva Product Recalls
item.description:
• Summary: Viatris and Teva face separate product recalls: Viatris for extended-release Xanax (alprazolam) and Teva for a blood pressure patch, underscoring manufacturing quality issues.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma

item.title: Biotech Funding & Deal Trends
item.description:
• Summary: Trends show explosive growth in obesity deals, with Q1 2026 exceeding total 2025 value, while early-stage funding slumps toward post-pandemic lows, pressuring biotech startups. European efforts to scale science into commercial success are also discussed.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Biospace, Fierce Biotech, Biospace
